name: Lac d'Agès Loop
name_original: Tour du Lac d'Agès – course à pied
description: Jog the moderate Lac d'Agès Loop, a 3.2-mile trail offering lake views, wooded banks, and birdwatching opportunities near Hagetmau.
region_intro: Jogging around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur offers a network of routes primarily centered around Lac d'Agès and the surrounding countryside near Hagetmau. The terrain is generally flat to gently undulating, with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for various fitness levels. These running routes often feature lakeside paths and trails through open fields, providing accessible options for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur The most popular jogging route is Lac d'Agès Loop, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes 31 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic path around the lake. Another top favourite among local runners is Lac d'Agès loop from Hagetmau, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route extends from Hagetmau, incorporating more of the local landscape around the lake. Local runners also love the Château d'Agès loop from Hagetmau, a 5.8 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through the countryside near the Château d'Agès, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Jogging around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur is defined by lakeside paths, open countryside, and gentle slopes. The network includes 3 easy jogging routes, 20 moderate running routes, and 1 difficult trail, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur?
A: There are over 20 running routes available in the Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur area. These include 3 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 1 difficult trail, offering options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in the region?
A: Yes, the area features 3 easy jogging routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. The terrain around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur is generally flat to gently undulating, making it very accessible. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001100571/lac-d-ages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Agès Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.2 miles (5.1 km) and offers a scenic, gentle path.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running routes around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur?
A: Running routes in this area vary in length. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001100571/lac-d-ages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Agès Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 3.2 miles (5.1 km) and takes around 31 minutes. Longer options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crypt Saint-Girons – Lac d'Agès loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; extend to about 9 miles (14.6 km), taking roughly 1 hour 40 minutes.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001100571/lac-d-ages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Agès Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29087527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château d'Agès loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples of popular circular trails.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails?
A: The running trails around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur offer diverse scenery, primarily featuring picturesque lakeside paths around Lac d'Agès and trails winding through open fields and the surrounding countryside. You'll experience gentle slopes and natural landscapes characteristic of the Landes region.

Q: Are the running routes around Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur suitable for families?
A: Yes, the generally flat to gently undulating terrain and the presence of easy routes make many of the paths suitable for families. The scenic lakeside paths, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001100571/lac-d-ages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Agès Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are particularly pleasant for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in rural areas like Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage for any specific restrictions, particularly around protected natural areas or private property.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Many routes, especially those starting from Hagetmau or around Lac d'Agès, offer convenient parking options. For instance, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Agès loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; typically have accessible starting points with nearby parking facilities in or around the town.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lac D'Hagetmau-Monségur?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil lakeside paths, the accessible terrain suitable for all levels, and the pleasant countryside views. The well-maintained trails around Lac d'Agès are frequently highlighted as a favorite.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running routes?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, there is 1 difficult trail available, and several moderate routes extend further into the countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24583530" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sainte-Colombe&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a moderate 6-mile (9.6 km) route with more elevation gain, offering a good workout.

Q: Are there any points of interest or landmarks along the running routes?
A: While there are no specific highlights listed, some routes pass by notable local features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29087527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château d'Agès loop from Hagetmau&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the countryside near the historic Château d'Agès, offering a glimpse of local heritage amidst your run.
tips: Although it serves to support the region during low water and is fed by local springs, the lake is mainly used for fishing. Swimming is prohibited, but walking is encouraged thanks to a pretty path around the lake.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Lac d'Agès Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, the trail is easily accessible and popular, suggesting designated parking is available near the lake. Look for signs for pedestrian access points around Lac d'Agès, especially near Hagetmau.

faqs.question: Is the Lac d'Agès Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is considered easy and presents no particular technical difficulty, especially in good weather. Its gentle slopes and well-defined path make it suitable for all ages and fitness levels, including families and beginner joggers.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a well-marked pedestrian path that circles the lake. It is largely flat, with only a few short, small ascents (around four in total). The path can be stony in parts and potentially clayey and slippery when wet, so appropriate footwear is recommended.

faqs.question: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy diverse scenery, staying very close to Lac d'Agès with numerous water views. The path features undulating and wooded banks, including a beautiful eucalyptus forest on the eastern side. It also passes between the main lake and a wilder pond, offering opportunities for birdwatching, especially in winter.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog the Lac d'Agès Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round. However, for the most comfortable jogging experience, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures. Winter can be good for birdwatching, but be mindful that the path might be clayey and slippery when wet.

faqs.question: Is the Lac d'Agès Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, making it a great option for exercising with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, and be mindful of other users and potential wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog the Lac d'Agès Loop. It is a publicly accessible recreational area.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a shaded picnic area available, making it a good spot for a longer outing or a post-jog rest. Information on restrooms is not specified, so it's advisable to plan accordingly.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Lac d'Agès Loop by jogging?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, completing the approximately 5.1 km loop typically takes around 30-35 minutes, depending on your pace and any stops you might make.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the potential for stony or slippery sections, appropriate jogging footwear with good grip is recommended. Dress in layers suitable for the weather, and consider bringing water, especially on warmer days. A camera might be nice for the scenic views.

faqs.question: Is the Lac d'Agès Loop a popular trail, and can it get crowded?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Lac d'Agès Loop is a popular spot for exercise and relaxation, particularly for joggers. While it's a well-loved route, its open nature generally accommodates visitors without feeling overly crowded, especially during off-peak hours.
